title: Leakage-safe latent degradation state features
summary: Recent F1 research models tyre pace as a latent state with pit stops as structural resets and shows rolling-origin gains over AR baselines.
rationale: Recent F1 research models tyre pace as a latent state with pit stops as structural resets and shows rolling-origin gains over AR baselines. That suggests the current handcrafted degradation proxies are missing a hidden state capturing pace loss beyond raw `TyreLife` and `Cumulative_Degradation`.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race_Year-Driver-Stint`, fit only backward-looking local state features such as EWMA pace loss, residualized lap-time delta after race-lap median removal, slope/acceleration of degradation, and post-reset recovery depth; feed those latent-state proxies into the existing tabular model.
expected_effect: Should sharpen decision boundaries near true cliff laps and distinguish long but stable stints from long and collapsing stints.
risk: Easy to leak if the smoother uses future laps; keep all calculations strictly causal.
